title: usePrevious done correctly with stable reference
scenario_context: Need previous value of a prop to detect change direction or animate transition. Naive implementations have stale closures or wrong timing.
verification_chain: Layer 8: ref updated in useEffect after render
Layer 8: returns undefined on first render
Layer 9: consumer can compare prev != current safely
Layer 10: assertion telemetry and regression alert
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Updating ref in render — multi-render issues
Returning ref.current AFTER updating — returns current not prev
useState + setState in effect — extra render
what_breaks_naive_approach: Animating count change: prev wrong direction shown.
reasoning: useRef holds value. useEffect updates AFTER render commits. Hook returns ref.current BEFORE the update — which is previous value.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for generic_react_hooks; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.
error_handling_strategy.telemetry: generic_react_hooks_error_total, denied_total, retry_total, and assertion-specific counters.

edge_cases_addressed: Object reference equality — caller compares correctly
Async value updates — effect runs after commit
test_strategy: Render with sequence 1,2,3; assert prev is undef,1,2.
observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-061, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Ref + effect, not state. 2) Returns undefined on first render — explicit. 3) Single line correct implementation.

title: useUpdateEffect: skip first render
scenario_context: Side effect should run only when deps change, not on mount. Common for analytics tracking, debounced search input, etc.
verification_chain: Layer 8: effect skips when first mount
Layer 9: deps change after mount → effect runs
Layer 10: assertion telemetry and regression alert
anti_patterns_to_avoid: setTimeout to defer first effect — race
Manual useState for hasMounted — extra render
what_breaks_naive_approach: Search input fires API call on mount with empty query.
reasoning: useRef tracks isFirst. Effect callback checks; if first, set false and return; else run.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for generic_react_hooks; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.
error_handling_strategy.telemetry: generic_react_hooks_error_total, denied_total, retry_total, and assertion-specific counters.

edge_cases_addressed: StrictMode double-effect — ref reset properly between mounts
Deps array referential change — same as useEffect
Cleanup function — returned from inner effect
test_strategy: Render with deps unchanged → no run. Change deps → runs.
observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-062, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Tiny but consistently misimplemented. 2) Ref over state — no extra render. 3) Cleanup function honored.

title: useResizeObserver with debounce and ref-target
scenario_context: Component reacts to container size changes (charts, virtualized lists). ResizeObserver fires per frame on drag; debounce avoids storms.
verification_chain: Layer 8: hook returns ref + size state
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Reading getBoundingClientRect in render — layout thrash
what_breaks_naive_approach: Window resize listener doesn't fire when sidebar collapses → chart wrong size.
reasoning: ResizeObserver on element. requestAnimationFrame batches. Debounce optional via flag. Returns size or null initially.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for generic_react_hooks; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.
error_handling_strategy.telemetry: generic_react_hooks_error_total, denied_total, retry_total, and assertion-specific counters.

edge_cases_addressed: Container with overflow — observe self not children
test_strategy: Resize mock; debounced fires once after burst.
observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-063, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) ResizeObserver > resize event. 2) Debounce optional. 3) Returns null until first observation — consumer handles.

title: useFormField as generic primitive: value + error + dirty + touched
scenario_context: Building custom form library without external deps. Each field is a state machine: pristine → dirty → touched → validated.
verification_chain: Layer 8: hook returns props for input + state
Layer 9: blur marks touched; change marks dirty
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Single form-state object — re-render entire form on any keystroke
No touched concept — error on first focus
what_breaks_naive_approach: User opens form; every field shows red 'required' before they typed.
reasoning: Per-field hook with value, error, dirty, touched. Validation runs on blur (or on change after first blur). showError = touched && error.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Server errors mapped via separate setError

edge_cases_addressed: First-blur-then-edit pattern — validate on change after
test_strategy: Test value/touched/dirty transitions.
observability_hooks: Track which fields users most invalidate
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Touched gate prevents error spam. 2) Per-field isolation avoids re-render. 3) inputProps spread for ergonomics.

title: Domain-specific error hierarchy for payment processing
scenario_context: Payment failures have many causes (card declined, fraud, insufficient funds, 3DS challenge). Generic 500 hides them. Need typed errors that map to specific UX.
verification_chain: Layer 3: service throws specific subclass per Stripe error type
Layer 4: middleware serializes with code + customer-safe message
Layer 6: client maps to specific UI
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Catching all Stripe errors as generic
Showing raw Stripe error to user — exposes internals
what_breaks_naive_approach: 3DS challenge returned as generic 'payment failed' — user gives up. Correct: trigger 3DS flow.
reasoning: Map Stripe error types: card_declined → PaymentDeclinedError; requires_action → ThreeDSecureRequiredError; insufficient_funds → variant; etc. Each has UX strategy.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Subclass per Stripe error type

edge_cases_addressed: Network drop during charge — paymentIntent status check on retry
Duplicate charge — idempotency key per intent
Webhook ordering — payment_intent.succeeded may arrive before charge response
test_strategy: Stripe test cards for each failure mode. Assert correct UI per code.
observability_hooks: Per-code rate — high CARD_DECLINED may indicate fraud surge
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Typed errors per Stripe failure. 2) 3DS handled gracefully. 3) Customer-safe messages — no Stripe internals leaked.

title: Offline detection with real-internet probe (not just navigator.onLine)
scenario_context: navigator.onLine returns true if connected to wifi without internet. Need real probe to detect actual connectivity.
verification_chain: Layer 7: online state derived from probe + navigator.onLine
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Banner when single request fails — overreaction
what_breaks_naive_approach: User on captive portal wifi — navigator.onLine true, no real internet. App appears broken but doesn't say why.
reasoning: Probe a known-good endpoint (your own /health or 1x1 pixel) on fetch failure. If probe fails, declare offline. Re-probe periodically. Listen for navigator.onLine for fast-path. Use ServiceWorker for advanced offline.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for error_handling_client; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.

edge_cases_addressed: Captive portal — probe fails despite navigator.onLine
Slow network — probe times out, treated offline
Server health endpoint down (rare) — false offline; consider second probe
ServiceWorker cached health response — bypass with cache:no-store
test_strategy: Mock navigator.onLine + fetch responses. Assert online state transitions.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Real probe vs navigator.onLine. 2) Re-probe schedule with backoff. 3) UI assures user data won't be lost.

title: tRPC-style shared contracts without tRPC: typed RPC over fetch
scenario_context: Want end-to-end types without tRPC's runtime. Define route map shared between server + client; generate typed client.
verification_chain: Layer 5: route map exported from server
Layer 6: client wraps map, returns typed fetchers
what_breaks_naive_approach: Rename endpoint, forget to update client; types pass; runtime fails.
reasoning: Server exports RouteMap type. Client uses TypeScript inference to create typed client. Zod schemas validate at runtime. Compile error if endpoint changes shape.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Zod errors → ValidationError; output schema mismatch → 500 (programmer error)

edge_cases_addressed: Add new procedure — type appears on rpc.* immediately
Rename procedure — TS error at all call sites
Schema change — runtime parse fails fast
No tRPC server runtime needed — standard Express
Versioning — namespace by version in route key
test_strategy: TS-only test: add procedure, autocomplete works. Runtime: invalid input rejected.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) End-to-end types without runtime dependency. 2) Server validates input + output — drift caught. 3) Proxy gives ergonomic API.

title: Tenant-aware cache with explicit invalidation
scenario_context: Cache expensive query results per tenant. Naive global cache leaks across tenants. Per-tenant cache with isolated keys and invalidation events.
verification_chain: Layer 3: invalidate fires across instances via pub/sub
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Global cache key without tenant prefix
Stale cache after write — manual invalidation forgotten
what_breaks_naive_approach: Tenant A's data cached under 'projects_list'. Tenant B reads — sees A's data.
reasoning: Cache wrapper auto-prefixes with tenant. Writes through specific service methods call invalidate. Redis pub/sub broadcasts invalidations to all instances. SingleFlight prevents stampede.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Cache miss on Redis error → fall through to loader
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.

edge_cases_addressed: Tenant switch via impersonation — context provides target tenant
Pub/sub partition — local cache stale → TTL bounds error
Cross-tenant invalidation — Wildcard scan dangerous; use prefix-based delete
test_strategy: Concurrent gets — one loader call. Invalidate — next get reloads. Cross-tenant — keys distinct.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Tenant prefix at choke point — no leakage. 2) Singleflight prevents stampede. 3) Pub/sub for multi-instance invalidation.

title: Sample data seeding for empty accounts
scenario_context: New tenant signs up. Dashboard is empty — looks broken. Seed sample data so they see what the app can do. Must be clearly marked + easy to remove.
verification_chain: Layer 1: sample entities tagged with isSample
Layer 4: GET filters can exclude sample
Layer 8: badge on sample items; 'Remove samples' CTA
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Real-looking sample data — user confused which is theirs
what_breaks_naive_approach: User wonders 'who is Alice?'. Sample data needs 'Sample' badge.
reasoning: isSample boolean on records. Bulk-delete endpoint. UI badge. After user creates first real item, suggest removing samples. Don't count toward quota.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Per-op

edge_cases_addressed: Sample referenced by user's real item — keep
test_strategy: Signup → samples present. Create real item → banner appears. Remove → samples gone.
observability_hooks: Conversion: % users who remove vs keep
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Badged clearly. 2) Quota-exempt. 3) Edit promotes to real — natural transition.

title: Custom Recharts shape for data points with annotations
scenario_context: Funnel chart: each stage is a step, drop-off annotated. Default dot isn't enough — need bigger circle, line to label, hover halo.
verification_chain: Layer 8: custom Dot component receives cx, cy, payload
Layer 10: assertion telemetry and regression alert
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Inline SVG in <Dot> via JSX strings
what_breaks_naive_approach: Default dot too small; users miss data points.
reasoning: <Line> dot prop accepts function. Component receives positions + data. Render SVG circle + text + line.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for recharts_mastery; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.
error_handling_strategy.telemetry: recharts_mastery_error_total, denied_total, retry_total, and assertion-specific counters.

edge_cases_addressed: Long stage names — XAxis tick rotation
test_strategy: Storybook with funnel data. Visual check.
observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-070, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Custom Dot for richer annotations. 2) Conditional drop-off label. 3) ActiveDot for hover affordance.

title: Visx voronoi for dense scatter with hover-anywhere interactivity
scenario_context: 10k point scatter; hovering finds nearest point. Naive distance check on every mousemove is slow.
verification_chain: Layer 8: pointer events on overlay rect
Layer 9: nearest point found in O(log n)
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Distance check on every point — O(n) per mousemove
what_breaks_naive_approach: 10k points × mousemove × distance — janky.
reasoning: d3-voronoi precomputes diagram once per data change. Overlay rect captures pointer. .find(x,y) returns nearest in log time. Highlight nearest via hover state.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for visx_d3; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.

test_strategy: Performance budget: 10k points, hover < 16ms
observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-071, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Voronoi for O(log n) hover. 2) Memoized scales. 3) Radius limit for far points.

title: Chart.js plugin for vertical reference line annotation
scenario_context: Annotate event on time-series (deploy time, incident start). Chart.js supports plugins for custom drawing.
verification_chain: Layer 8: plugin registered globally or per chart
Layer 10: assertion telemetry and regression alert
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Annotation via DOM overlay — misaligned on resize
Plugin shared with state mutation — leaks
what_breaks_naive_approach: DOM overlay desyncs on resize.
reasoning: chartjs-plugin-annotation handles common cases. For custom: plugin afterDatasetsDraw, get xScale, draw vertical line at event timestamp.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for chartjs_imperative; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.
error_handling_strategy.telemetry: chartjs_imperative_error_total, denied_total, retry_total, and assertion-specific counters.

test_strategy: Visual check with annotations at various x.
observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-072, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Plugin draws on canvas — stays aligned. 2) Skip out-of-area. 3) Restore ctx — no side effects.

title: ECharts gauge chart with thresholds and color-coded ranges
scenario_context: SLA dashboard: 99.9% target. Gauge shows current uptime with green/yellow/red zones.
verification_chain: Layer 8: gauge series with axis ranges
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Gauge for general metrics (excess chart junk)
what_breaks_naive_approach: Single color gauge — user doesn't know if 99.5% is good.
reasoning: axisLine color stops define zones. Pointer color matches current zone. Use sparingly — gauges only for bounded metrics with clear thresholds.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Per-page
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.

edge_cases_addressed: Color blind — second visual cue (text label per zone)
test_strategy: Storybook various values; assert zone color.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Threshold-aware coloring. 2) Sparingly used — only for bounded metrics. 3) aria-label for SR.

title: Tremor dashboard layout that's responsive and printable
scenario_context: Exec dashboard needs to look good on desktop + tablet + print to PDF for board meetings.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No print styles — random page breaks
Hidden elements on print but layout shift
what_breaks_naive_approach: Print: cards split across pages, charts cut off, navigation appears.
reasoning: CSS Grid with grid-template-columns minmax for fluid. @media print hides nav, sets page-break-inside: avoid on cards, expands columns to full width.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for tremor_dashboards; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.

edge_cases_addressed: Dark mode print — light bg forced
test_strategy: Visual regression per breakpoint. Print preview in Chrome.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Print is a first-class layout. 2) break-inside-avoid keeps cards intact. 3) Force light bg for ink-saving.

title: Single data point chart — switch to big-number display
scenario_context: Trend chart with only one data point looks broken. Detect single-point case, switch to big-number widget.
verification_chain: Layer 7: state branches on point count
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Force-render line with single point — invisible
what_breaks_naive_approach: Single point becomes nothing visible. Big number with date is more honest.
reasoning: If points.length === 1, render big-number with explanation 'trend chart appears after 2+ points'. Same container height to avoid layout shift.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for chart_ux_edge_cases; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.

edge_cases_addressed: Two points — minimal trend, line visible
Many points all same value — flat line ok
Many points spanning long time — date axis scaled
test_strategy: Storybook each count.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Adaptive rendering by data shape. 2) Same height — no layout shift. 3) Teach when trend will appear.

title: Changing a validation rule across both sides simultaneously
scenario_context: Increase password min length from 8 to 12. Naive: change server only. Old client still allows 8, submits, gets 400. Or change client only — server still accepts 8, weak passwords slip in.
verification_chain: Layer 0: shared Zod schema with min length
Layer 8: client form uses zodResolver with same schema
Layer 9: existing users still log in (rule applies at change time)
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Forcing existing users to re-set instantly
what_breaks_naive_approach: Mismatch causes 400s on submit; existing users logged out by force.
reasoning: Shared schema is single source. New rule applies to NEW passwords. Existing users prompted to update at next login. Telemetry tracks compliance.
shared_schema: // shared/schemas/password.ts\nimport { z } from 'zod';\nexport const PasswordSchema = z.string().min(12, 'At least 12 characters').refine(p => /[A-Z]/.test(p), 'Needs uppercase').refine(p => /[0-9]/.test(p), 'Needs digit');

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Validation failure same as before

edge_cases_addressed: Existing users — flagged for upgrade, not forced
Password manager autofill — works as before
test_strategy: Schema test. Old password works until upgrade. Banner shown.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Single Zod schema — rules can't drift. 2) Grace period for existing users. 3) Telemetry tracks compliance, not assumption.

title: What if the server returns 200 with malformed body — fail loud, not silent
scenario_context: Bug or proxy injects HTML into JSON response. JSON.parse fails. Naive: catches and uses defaults — feature silently broken.
verification_chain: Layer 6: parse failure throws typed error
anti_patterns_to_avoid: catch + empty array — feature gone, no signal
what_breaks_naive_approach: Proxy interstitial returns HTML; JSON.parse fails; empty array; user sees 'No items'; no clue.
reasoning: Check content-type. Parse fails → throw with sample. Sentry captures. UI shows error not empty.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Same as malformed

test_strategy: Mock fetch returning HTML; assert throw with sample.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Fail loud not silent. 2) Content-type check + sample in error. 3) Empty array as fallback masks bugs — avoid.

title: Coerced types in Zod: dates, numbers from query strings
scenario_context: Query strings are all strings. Form data may be strings. Zod's z.coerce handles conversion at the boundary cleanly.
verification_chain: Layer 10: assertion telemetry and regression alert
what_breaks_naive_approach: ?from=2025-01-01 → trusted as Date → arithmetic breaks.
reasoning: z.coerce.date(), z.coerce.number(), z.coerce.boolean() at boundaries. Schema enforces both shape and type.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Validation per field
error_handling_strategy.telemetry: end_to_end_type_safety_error_total, denied_total, retry_total, and assertion-specific counters.

edge_cases_addressed: Limit > max — capped via .max with error
Multiple values for same key — Zod errors
test_strategy: Query each shape; assert parsed or rejected.
observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-078, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Coerce at boundary, not throughout. 2) Single schema for both URL building and parsing. 3) Refinements catch business rules.

title: Cross-tenant data isolation tests in CI
scenario_context: Automated test suite ensures NO endpoint leaks data across tenants. Property test: random tenant pair, no method returns the other's data.
verification_chain: Test infrastructure: fixture creates two tenants with data
Test runs every list/get/search endpoint as both tenants
CI gate: PR blocked if test fails
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Trusting reviewers to check tenant isolation
Test in same tenant — misses leakage
what_breaks_naive_approach: New endpoint without tenant filter ships — found in prod by accident.
reasoning: Set up two tenants with overlapping IDs. Run every endpoint as tenant A. Assert response contains no tenant B IDs. Run as B, assert no A. Generic test harness iterates over OpenAPI spec.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for saas_multi_tenancy; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.

edge_cases_addressed: Background jobs — separate test runs job in tenant context
Admin endpoints — separate isolation per admin scope
Search — full-text indexes filter by tenant
Exports — tenant scope on export trigger
test_strategy: Run weekly even outside PR. Mutation testing — break filter, assert test catches.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Automated proof of isolation. 2) OpenAPI-driven test coverage. 3) Mutation testing catches missing checks.

title: Invoice presentation with line items and tax breakdown
scenario_context: User views invoice. Naive: shows total. Customer needs full breakdown with proration, taxes, discounts, payment method.
verification_chain: Layer 8: rich UI with line items
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Linking to Stripe's invoice — leaves app
Hardcoded tax rate — varies by jurisdiction
No PDF download — customer needs for accounting
what_breaks_naive_approach: User can't reconcile invoice to expectations — support ticket.
reasoning: Mirror Stripe invoice structure: line items, subtotals, tax lines, discounts, total. Generate PDF server-side. Show payment status + method.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: NotFound if wrong customer

test_strategy: Stripe test invoice fixture; render full breakdown.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Full breakdown not just total. 2) PDF download for accounting. 3) Period dates on line items.

title: Permission caching with cross-instance invalidation
scenario_context: Permission lookups on every request slow. Cache in memory. Need invalidation when admin changes a role.
verification_chain: Layer 3: role change publishes invalidate event
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No cache — DB hit per request
what_breaks_naive_approach: Admin demotes user. User keeps admin access until cache TTL.
reasoning: Cache user role + grants. On role change, publish via Redis pub/sub. All instances invalidate. Local cache with short TTL as safety net.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: DB miss → cached empty briefly; Redis failure → cache works locally
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Stale UI on race — server still enforces
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.

edge_cases_addressed: User logged in multiple sessions — invalidate hits all
Role change while in-flight request — server re-checks
Test mode — disable cache for determinism
test_strategy: Concurrent invalidation across mock instances.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) TTL bounds staleness even if pub/sub fails. 2) Multi-instance invalidation. 3) Cache invalidation on role change is the bug source.

title: Recharts radar chart for multi-dimension comparison
scenario_context: Compare two products across 6 dimensions. Radar chart shows shape at a glance. Naive: no axes scale per dimension.
verification_chain: Layer 8: each axis can have different scale
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Same scale all axes — distorts when units differ
what_breaks_naive_approach: Radar of (revenue, users, churn rate) on 0-100 scale — revenue dominates.
reasoning: Normalize per axis to 0-100 for visual. Show absolute in tooltip. Limit to ≤8 axes. Use radar only when shape comparison matters.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for recharts_mastery; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.
error_handling_strategy.telemetry: recharts_mastery_error_total, denied_total, retry_total, and assertion-specific counters.

test_strategy: Storybook with various data.
observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-082, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Normalize for visual + raw for tooltip. 2) Limit to 2-3 series. 3) Per-axis max.

title: Chart.js with WebGL backend (chartjs-chart-graph) for massive scatter
scenario_context: 100k+ scatter points; canvas backend reaches limits. WebGL backend via specific plugins handles bigger.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Single bundle includes WebGL for small charts
what_breaks_naive_approach: 100k canvas points = 5fps. WebGL = 60fps.
reasoning: Specific Chart.js plugins (or D3 + regl) provide WebGL. Implement context-loss handler. Downsample for hover via voronoi.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for chartjs_imperative; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.

test_strategy: Perf budget; context loss simulation.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Right tool for scale. 2) Context-loss handling. 3) Decimation.

title: ECharts theme system with light/dark/custom
scenario_context: Multi-tenant dashboard branding. Each tenant has its colors. Register themes per tenant.
verification_chain: Layer 7: theme name from tenant config
what_breaks_naive_approach: Tenant primary blue, chart series blue — no contrast.
reasoning: echarts.registerTheme(name, themeObj). themeObj defines color palette + axis styles. Re-init chart on theme change.
shared_schema: // shared/schemas/theme.ts\nimport { z } from 'zod';\nexport const ThemeSchema = z.object({ name: z.string(), colors: z.array(z.string()), backgroundColor: z.string().optional(), textStyle: z.object({ color: z.string() }).optional() });

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Missing theme → default
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.

test_strategy: Visual snapshot per theme
observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-084, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Theme as data. 2) Registered once. 3) Tenant-specific.

title: Date range with sparse data — adaptive aggregation
scenario_context: User selects 1-year range. Daily aggregation = 365 points (fine). User selects 3-year range = 1095 points (still ok but maybe weekly is clearer). 10-year range = 3650 — too dense.
verification_chain: Layer 3: server picks aggregation level based on range
what_breaks_naive_approach: 10-year daily chart loads 3650 points; user can't see annual trends.
reasoning: Server returns weekly for 1-3y, monthly for 3-10y, yearly for >10y. Chart shows aggregation level in legend ('weekly average').

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Standard
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.

edge_cases_addressed: Cross-boundary range (e.g., 89 days) — round to nearest
Sparse data even with weekly — explicit gap visualization
Live data — append new points at current resolution
test_strategy: Range edges: 90/91 days, 365/366. Resolution switches correctly.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Server picks resolution — readable charts. 2) Resolution surfaced in UI. 3) Pre-computed rollups for fast queries.

title: Service worker for offline + background sync
scenario_context: Offline-first PWA. Service worker caches assets, queues failed POSTs via Background Sync.
verification_chain: Layer 7: cached responses served when offline
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No SW update flow — stuck old version
what_breaks_naive_approach: User mutates offline; comes back online; data lost.
reasoning: Workbox or hand-rolled SW. CacheFirst for static, NetworkFirst for API. BackgroundSync queues mutations. Replay with idempotency key.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Idempotency

edge_cases_addressed: Auth token expired during replay — refresh
test_strategy: Cypress with offline mode.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Idempotency for replay safety. 2) Update flow. 3) NetworkFirst for API; CacheFirst for static.

title: Database returns success but client never gets response
scenario_context: User submits payment. DB commits. Network drops before response reaches client. Client times out, retries — duplicate charge.
verification_chain: Layer 6: client retries with same key
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No status endpoint for in-flight ops
what_breaks_naive_approach: Double-charge. Refund request. Customer angry.
reasoning: All POSTs with idempotency-key header. Server dedupes via cached response. Client status endpoint for ambiguous state.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Cached response on duplicate

test_strategy: Chaos test: kill connection at commit
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Idempotency at the right level. 2) Status check before retry. 3) Honest UX during ambiguity.

title: Generic Result type with andThen/map for chaining
scenario_context: Multi-step operation: validate, fetch, compute, save. Each can fail. Result chain avoids if-error-return ladders.
verification_chain: Layer 0: Result type with andThen, map
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Loss of error context across steps
what_breaks_naive_approach: 5-step service with 5 nested if-else — unreadable.
reasoning: Add helpers: ok.andThen(fn) chains if ok; ok.map(fn) transforms value. Errors short-circuit.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Per-step error with context

edge_cases_addressed: Error context not lost across steps
Refactor: add step in middle, types check
test_strategy: Each step failure path. End-to-end happy.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Chain ergonomics — readable multi-step ops. 2) Per-step error context. 3) TS forces handling at endpoints.

title: Per-tenant quotas with soft and hard limits
scenario_context: Free plan: 10 projects soft, 15 hard. Soft = warning, hard = block. Need fine-grained per-resource limits.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No way to upgrade in flow
what_breaks_naive_approach: User at 15 projects on free hits hard limit creating 16th — surprise. Soft at 10 lets them know in advance.
reasoning: Cached counter per (tenant, kind). Updated on create/delete. Soft = display warning. Hard = throw QuotaError with upgrade meta.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: QuotaError on hard limit

test_strategy: Hit soft → banner. Hit hard → error.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Soft + hard distinction — no surprises. 2) Atomic counter via Redis. 3) Upgrade CTA in error meta.

title: Two users hit same endpoint concurrently — race-safe with transactions
scenario_context: Two admins approve same pending invite simultaneously. Naive: both succeed; user joins twice or seat exceeded.
verification_chain: Layer 4: idempotent response on second arrival
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Polling for state in middle of op
what_breaks_naive_approach: Double-membership. Seat overflow.
reasoning: Wrap in DB transaction with serializable isolation OR unique constraint that catches duplicate. Application-level idempotency returns previous result.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: CONFLICT or success

test_strategy: Concurrent fire 10x; assert one succeeds, others get specific result.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Transaction + unique constraint defense in depth. 2) Idempotent response. 3) Honest UX on conflict.
